Dance Central - Basic Movements
The Samba Basic Movements is Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Samba Natural Basic Movement
Alignment is according to previous figures.
Leader
1: RF fwd | No turn or up to 1/4 to R over 1-6 | BF
See Samba Technique for detailed description of bounce action and foot movement rhythm.
Trailing LF: IE, roll ankle down.
a: Close LF to RF w/o weight | B (pressure)
2: take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
1: LF back | BF
front leg looks straight at this moment.
a: Close RF to LF w/o weight | B (pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF | BF
Follower
Alignment is according to previous figures.
1: LF back | No turn or up to 1/4 to R over 1-6 | BF
See Samba Technique for detailed description of bounce action and foot movement rhythm.
a: Close RF to LF w/o weight | B (pressure)
No weight on RF. Weight should still on LF.
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF | BF
1: RF fwd | BF
Bound action
Trailing LF: IE, roll ankle down.
a: Close LF to RF w/o weight | B (pressure)
"Pressure" step.
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
When used as a Precede to Traveling Bota Fogos Forward, start Natural Basic Movement facing LOD and turn 1/8 L over 2 and 3. 4-6 are then danced with follower outside.
When used as a Follow to Traveling Bota Fogos Forward, the first step is outside partner. Turn 1/8 R over 2 and 3 to face partner.
Precedes: any figure ending facing partner with weight on LF.
Follows: any figure commenced with RF.
Variation: 
Use minimal bounce and use 4 steps as below. follower does opposite. (Similar for Reverse Basic, Side Basic and Progressive Basic.)
1: RF fwd | BF
2: Close LF to RF with pressure , but without weight | BF
Keeping weight on RF
(Difference: instead of taking minimal weight onto LF and replace weight back to RF)
1: LF back | BF
2: Closed RF to LF with pressure but without weight | BF
Keeping weight on LF

Samba Side Basic Movement
This may be danced in place of the Natural or Reverse Basic taking the first step to side. All others are the same.
When taking side step to R side, frame to right, R knee bent, but leaving L hip to L side till the next "and", really stretch the body long without tilting shoulder; roll the hip to R (clock-wise). 2 rounds ending stepping to L.
When stepping to side, the free foot position is important: roll the foot down, IE of foot touch floor, not ball of foot.
Similarly, when taking step to L side, leave R hip to right till the next "and", roll the hip to L (counter clock-wise).

Samba Progressive Basic Movement
Leader: 1-3 step of natural basic (RF fwd), followed by 4-6 of side basic to L.
Follower: 1-3 step of natural basic (LF back), followed by 4-6 of side basic to R (During side basic, optionally, Follower can turn her head to left, toward LF.)
Alignment: DW, no turn.

Dance Central - Argentine Crosses
Samba Argentine Crosses
The Samba Argentine Crosses is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This figure starts in Closed Position. No Bounce.
Leader
Q: LF fwd, toe turned out | start to turn R | BF
Normally 1 turn. But up to 2 complete turns can be made over 12 steps.
Included body slightly L over steps 1-3, 7-9 and R over 4-6 and 10-12.
Q: RF behind LF (Cuban Cross) | 1/8 to R btwn 1 and 2 | B
S: LF to side and slightly fwd | 1/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | BF
Q: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | 1/4 to R btwn 3 and 4 | BF
Q: LF to side and slightly back | 1/8 to R btwn 4 and 5 | B
S: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | 1/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | BF
QQS QQS: Repeat steps 1-6 
Follower
Q: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | start to turn R | BF
Incline (shape) to R
Keep solid frame with Man, energy up, shoulder parallel to Man.
RF crosses in front once for steps 1-3, then crosses in back for steps 4-6 (if it's not underarm turn to L)
Q: LF to side and slightly back | 1/8 to R btwn 1 and 2 | B
S: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out. | 1/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | BF
Q: LF fwd, toe turned out | 1/4 to R btwn 3 and 4 | BF
Incline (shape) to L 
Variation: over steps 4 to 6, Man can turn Lady to R under raised L arm and then regain hold. Lady does 1 complete turn to R after closing her left side to Man.
Q: RF behind LF (Cuban Cross) | 1/8 to R btwn 4 and 5 | B
S: LF to side and slightly fwd | 1/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | BF
QQS QQS : Repeat steps 1-6 
Lady can do underarm turn over steps 10-12
Note:
Keep good frame with partner (more parallel)

Preceding steps: Alemana, Rope Spinning, Three Alemanas, Sliding Doors.
On last step, lead Lady towards R side then turn her R to R Shadow Position, get into one of the 3 R Shadow Holds.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to R on LF to end LF back.
count "2": Man and Lady then step back (Man LF, Lady RF)
count "3": Transfer weight fwd to RF (Lady LF)
count "4 1": LF fwd (Lady RF)
Follow with 6 Progressive Wlaks Forward with no turn or curving up to 1/2 to L. (Man start with RF, Lady LF)
Or dance Cuban Rock from Aida, then turn 1/2 (Man L, Lady R), taking R Shadow Position and hold, continue with 6 progressive Walks Forward as above.
When using Shadow Holds 2 or 3
count "4 1": Man could lead Lady to dance a spiral turn L when he has danced a Walk Forward on his LF (Lady RF fwd then make a complete turn to L on RF)
Continue with Progressive Walks Forward (Man starts RF, Lady LF)
Ending:
Fan Position: Man leading lady to pass in front of him to his L side, Man turns 1/8 L, Lady 7/8 L. When using Shadow hold 1, change to L to R hand hold on step 5 of Fan.
Ending facing partner: omit 6th Forward Walk and both step to side, turning 1/4 to R, Lady to L to face partner.
Spiral Ending: Omit 6th Forward Walk and close LF to RF leading Lady into a spiral turn to L.
Lady turn 1/4 L on the 6th walk to end in front of Man to dance her spiral turn.
Continue with 4-6 Spiral - this is solo spiral for Lady when R Shadow Hold 1 is used.

Dance Central - Rope Spinning
Rumba Rope Spinning
Rope Spinning is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Rope Spinning is commenced during the last step of the preceding figure. Man leads Lady to turn sharply R under raised L arm, inclining and turning his body slightly R, releasing R hand hold. Lady does Spiral, a complete turn to R on LF with foot flat (T of RF), body turning less, ending on Man's side facing opposite way. That's the Spiral Turn part. Man can keep his hand lightly in contact with Lady's back. What shows below is what follows the Spiral Turn.
Leader
2: LF to side | continue to turn body to R
Circle L hand clockwise over head over step 1 and 2
3: Transfer weight to RF | Turn body slightly to L
4 1: Close LF to RF in LSP | Continue to turn body to normal position
L hand to L side. Gradually lower to waist level
2: RF back | No turn
Continue to circle L hand clockwise over step 4 and 5. Incline body slightly L
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
return body to normal position.
4 1: Close RF to LF in Closed Position | --
Take required hold at end of this step.
Follower
2 3 4 1: Three walks fwd circling R behind Man's back RLR. End in LSP | 1/2 to R over step 1-3
2 3 4 1: Three walks fwd still circling R, LRL. End in Closed Position | 1/2 to R over step 4-6
Alternative finishing positions:
Man and Lady end to side
Man and lady end diagonally fwd in Open CPP
Man 1/8 R on step 6.
Lady 7/8 to R over step 1 to 6.
Lady end fwd toward Man's R side
Man turns body slightly R on step 6 to lead Lady towards his R side.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF.
Man and Lady fwd towards partner's R side
Man RF fwd in line with LF towards Lady's R side.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF
R Shadow Position
Man RF fwd in line with LF towards Lady's R side, and continues to turn her R.
Lady ends LF back having made 1/2 turn R on LF during preceding beat.
Adopt R shadow Hold.

Dance Central - Reverse Top
Rumba Reverse Top
This is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Start in Contact Position.
Leader
2: RF to side and slightly fwd | Up to 2 complete turns over steps 1-9
3: Swivel on ball of LF, ending LF in front of RF (Cuban Cross)
Man leave L toe in place, Lady rotates around it.
4 1: RF to side and slightly fwd
2 3 4 1 2 3 4 1: Repeat steps 2 and 3 three times
Follower
2: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross) | Up to 2 complete turns over steps 1-9
Make sure frame is good, solid, with energy. shoulder down. parallel to Man.
3: RF back and slightly to side
4 1: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross)
2 3 4 1 2 3 4 1: Repeat step 2 and 3 three times.
Note:
Because of the continuous turn, the knees will not straighten fully on steps 1 through 8. Only a slight, natural hip movement is used.

Dance Central - Spiral
Rumba Spiral Turn
Spiral Turn is a Silver Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
It is danced by the Lady and is a turn made on RF turning L or on LF turning R, allowing the other foot to cross in front without weight.
Normally a spiral turn will end with ankles crossed although the cross in the Curl is looser.
Leader
Commence in Closed Position
2: LF to side in RSP | slight body turn to R
Start like Closed Hip Twist.
Increase tone and lower L arm to R at chest level. Widen R arm in hold to R side
3: Transfer weight to RF
Retain tone in arms
4 1: Close LF to RF | slight body turn to L to normal position
Return arms to normal position and raise L hand above Lady's R shoulder to circle counterclock wise.
If preferred, Man can keep his fingers lightly in contact with Lady's waist as she dances her spiral turn.
2: RF back
L arm to L side lowering L hand to waist level
3: Transfer weight to LF | 1/8 to L over step 5 and 6
Increase tone in L arm
4 1: RF to side in Fan Position
Slightly extend L arm L hand still.
Could do either Aida ending or ending in Fan Position.
Follower
2: RF back in RSP | 1/2 to R
Start like Closed Hip Twist
Turn is commenced at end of preceding step.
3: Transfer weight to LF
Need to dance L hip more forward, don't be afraid to go forward, before rotating.
4 1: RF fwd and slightly across LF in front of Man, then turn underarm, allowing LF to cross in front without weight (Spiral Turn) | 1/4 to L, then a further 7/8 spiral turn
Lady can use extended forward walk.
Just turn 1/4 to L first, RF fwd, then spiral in front of Man
Turn is made on ball of RF with foot flat (T of LF) after getting full weight onto RF.
Turn could also be made on the R toe with heel well off the floor. (High Spiral)
At end of 1, make sure to settle R hip before stepping next step (shouldn't fall into next step.)
L arm elbow do not drop down, keep it up horizontal to keep volume/space.
L arm in front, keep volume.
2: LF fwd | 1/8 to L
3: RF fwd and slightly across, then turn to end RF back | 1/8 to L, then a further 1/2 Body turns less
Aida ending: RF fwd in line with LF, then 1/2 turn to L to end RF back in LSP.
4 1: LF back in Fan Position | Body completes turn
Aida ending: LF back in LSP.
Note: 
Alternatives:
Underturned Spiral Turn:
Man: turn 1/8 L over step 5 and 6 to end in Open Position with RF fwd.
Lady 3/4 L after she has placed step 3. End in Tandem Position with Man; Lady LF fwd,; RF fwd in line with LF, then turn 1/2 to end with RF back; then LF back.
Ending in Open Position:

Rumba Spot Turns and Switch Turns
These are Newcomer Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This is similar to Cha Cha Cha Spot Turns (including Switch Turns, Underarm Turns) (replacing chasse with one step).

2: RF fwd (after turning 1/4 to L on preceding "&") in line with LF. Then turn 1/2 to end with RF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to L, step RF, then 1/2 to L
This is Switch Turn to Left.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to LF
"3&", on "&": turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
At end of "1": turn to R.
2: LF fwd (after 1/4 to R) in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to end with LF in the back | 1/4 to R, step LF, then 1/2 to R
This is Switch Turn to Right.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to RF
on "&", turn 1/4 to R
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Follower
2: LF fwd (after turning 1/4 to R on preceding "&") in line with RF, Then turn 1/2 to R to end with LF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to R, step LF, Then 1/2 to R on "&"
This is Switch Turn to Left (because Man is turning L)
End of preceding step on '&', full commit the weight on RF, settle, pressure on LF, energy is forward, tail bone down; then swivel on RF, do quick impulse, turn 1/4 to R. Pay attention to foot position, LF turned out, not turned in (sometimes due to over turning to R on this step.)
Different from New Yorker: Fully commit weight on front foot before doing the half turn to R using Forward Walk Turn action.. (Difference in lead compared to New Yorker.)
Arm option: left hand on waist, or left arm wrap around the back. R arm can be held in front of tummy, palm facing tummy. Hands should not have other extra unintended actions, go directly to waist, pure and simple (if that's the choice).
On "&" after the turn, compress on left side. (Finishing with hips to the side, then, quick impulse, it will twist to move spine to RF in next step.)
To achieve the effect of "quick impulse" - sudden action:
body turns more continuously at same speed,
head: do head spotting and fast changing head direction
leg/knee delays the turn a bit and then turn much faster and body and leg arrives at the final position at the same time.
3: Transfer weight on to RF
Options:
before "3", there is a moment the R leg is bent, then straighten before stepping on floor.
or: Keep R leg straight the whole time.
Hold this a bit longer and have weight fully transfer to RF (see Latin Technique Swivel Action) and make sure to finish the diagonal pendulum swing before doing twist on '&'.
On "&", quick impulse, twist, turn 1/4 to R (turn before stepping next step)
When turning to right, it's Right Side leading.
Styling: when moving forward, arms would go back (think of wind blowing at the arms.)
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Turn/swivel at end of previous beat. Track LF underneath and then place under the shoulder to the side, not too big step. It's a smooth step, do not pause in middle. "4" is a side step (after the turning to face partner), do not make it a forward step, then turn. 
At end of this step, work RF down to ground, develop the foot more.
For underarm turn, at the "&": twist L hip, pulling R hip through, R arm will close in front of face (to left side). Keep L shoulder fwd side leading.
2: RF fwd (after 1/4 to L) in line with LF, Then turn 1/2 to L ending with RF in the back | 1/4 to L, then 1/2 to L
Switch Turn to Right (Man is turning to R)
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
When turning at the end of "2", body is not square, use Left Side Leading.
If Lady's R arm is connected to Man, Lady will feel R arm closing window.
Static body design: hands statically on hips while turning and extend arms to side on 41.
More flexible and fluid design: When turning, L arm extend to the side and as body rotate, arm will warp in front of tummy, R hand on hip or R arm wrap around the back.
For Switch Turns, if Man is still hold Lady's R hand, Lady: Put LH on hip first, (end of previous beat) then raising L arm to side during "2", at beginning of count "3", L arm goes in front of chest. (and Man will release hold to Lady's RH, Lady's R arm wrap around back.)
3: Transfer weight to LF 
on "&", turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
When followed by underarm turn or Alemana, L arm will be straight out to the side.
Note:
Spot Turns: 3 forward steps danced solo by Man and/or Lady, circling to L or R.
Spot Turns can be danced as Switch Turns. Start in Closed Position. No hold, or L to R or R to L hand hold.
Turning action: could be a Forward Walk Turn action, or a brushing action where the free foot closes to standing foot, brush and then do a fwd walk.
Different from underarm turn, for spot turns,
count "2": lady turns about 1/8", diagonally "&" RF brush LF
count "3": lady turns about 1/4, RF fwd
if preferred, the ball of the foot may remain in place while turn is made around this foot.
count "4 1": finish turn to face partner
last step could be fwd or to side depending on distance between couple.
Often, one partner is doing Spot Turn, while the other does Alternative Basic or Cuban Rock, or 3 steps of a Closed Basic.
Spot Turn is a common ending for New York, Hand to Hand, Fencing and may other figures.
Amount of Turns: complete turn, or 7/8 or 3/4, depending on starting and ending position: open PP, Open CPP, facing partner.
Spot Turn or Switch to L (Lady to R) could end in Open Position, taking L to R hand hold at step 3, taking step 3 fwd leading Lady to step back.

First Alemana, ending 2341, step 6: 
LF fwd in line with RF towards Man's R side, fwd step can be shorter. then turn to end LF back in RSP.
weight is onto whole LF faster on 4, giving it more stability and more time to twist to R. R hip high, open up to R. 
Then, more twisting to R, point RF, R leg very straight, the twisting hip to R makes left knee bend a little, but don't think too much about sitting butt out. The feeling is prepare to go into RF forward step, not having to get up from sitting position. 
R leg very straight, make the line while twisting to R. LF should swivel and turn enough. Upper body is winding up to get ready to turn the other direction (L)
create space in belly area, belly button to spine, release the R hip down, thighs tight together, upper body fwd. Keep head aligned with spine, don't twist or shape head. Create the K shape.
Twisting feels slower.
Lady L arm up while twisting (can also collect L arm in front of body, forearm vertical). R arm at center waist level, RH connected with Man, R elbow out (not down), While settling/rotating R hip to R, it creates pull tension with Man's LH, but do not pull R arm artificially. 
2: RF slightly leftwards, then turn underarm allowing LF to cross loosely in front of RF without weight (Spiral Turn) | 1/8 to L, then a further 3/4 to L
Small step, onto RF first (don't do shoulders yet), then turn shoulders.
Different styles of spiral turn: wrap foot (wing the foot, do not sickle foot), high spiral, locking ankles.
2 onto RF, options: do spiral: hold head, turning body to left, do a spiral turn, or direct fwd walk turn, pivot, then swivel.
Shoulder is to right, not turned completely flat.
Some do this more more diagonal, some more straight in front of Man. 
3: LF fwd | 1/8 to L
Almost feel like stepping away from Man. LF fwd underneath shoulder, keep very small steps, don't travel too much, do not step big outside body. 
Settle hip, Then, do flexible turn.
4 1: RF fwd | 1/2 to L
The turn is made at end of previous beat.
Pressed Fwd Walk. Lady should bring her weight well fwd, responding to Man's tone in his L arm. pressure in RH (to Man's LH) . 
Open L hip up, stretching the body (L hip back, R chest fwd to Man), release/drop R hip down.
L arm shoot out to L side.
2 3 4 1: Same as steps 4-6 of Alemana
A complete turn to R over steps 10-12.
Note:
There are a lot of turns/twists. Step onto foot, axis moved to the new standing foot, turn/twist around axis (shoulder first, hip follows), release free hip, move to new axis and start over.
One coach's imagery: rotisserie chicken on a skewer. The axis/skewer does not change, shift, break or move. chicken rotates around skewer.

Wheel on R elbow, R hand and upper arm behind head, do it completely, not half way; then elbow in front, then down to side of body 
4 1: RF fwd and across in TP | --

R leg/RF close to the standing LF first (R knee drape over) before it opens to pressed walk position. (Anna Kovalova's tip for Lady.)
RF leg is diagonally leftward. Do not step RF too big forward, make it more cross LF, more rotation to L.
RF needs to be turned out to R (turn knee out, R heel in to left & up) 
R leg is pressing fwd, L leg is back straight, stretching/creating space between the 2 legs. L hip rotated back L.
R side of body is forward, create diagonal stretch between L hip rotating back and R side of body stretching fwd.
Arms: Focus on working the body, not "working the hands". arm and hands movement comes from body movement. Arm movement to right syncs with when hip rotates to L.
R arm full arm to side, or 
R upper arm vertical next to body, flip lower arm to side like carrying a purse. Or,
R hand on hip. 
End of 1: Straight R leg, RF slide diagonally fwd, bigger R hip rotation which brings LF in, collect LF before stepping LF out (but seamless, no stopping LF).
2: LF to side | --

Option: Traditionally LF goes to side, current day, LF is taken more diagonally fwd to give it 3D (nice picture from every angle) and it makes next turn to R easier as well). 
L arm extend to left, squeeze side, full straight L arm action from side to front, leading with wrist, then hand follows. Then bent and collect L arm (don't bend too early). (R arm elbow in.) Cucaracha action

4 1: LF back and across in RSP | --

Cha Cha Cha Alemana
The Cha Cha Alemana is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Commencing positions:
Normally, in Fan Position 
May also be commenced in Open Position: Lady step back on step 1 instead of closing RF to LF, and makes a complete turn to R over 6-10.
Leader
2: LF fwd | No turn
3: Transfer weight to RF
4 & 1: Compact Chasse LRL
2: RF back
Man raises his L arm, table through elbow while Lady turns.
3: Transfer weight to LF
4 & 1: Compact Chasse RLR End in Closed Position
Follower
2: Closed RF to LF | No turn
Start from Fan Position. (In Open position, dance this step as back step.)
Fast knee switch. Compress R side. L knee drapes over the R knee. 
3: LF fwd
4 & 1: Fwd Lock RLR | 1/8 to R between Count & 1
Man's LH position may give hint to Lady whether it's going to be Hockey Stick or Alemana.
For Alemana: Lady's R hand/arm comes up and then opens out to right side.
On count "&" after 1: Do a sharp turn to R to Man's L side. LF in Latin Cross Position. Upper body opposition. Directly go into split weight.
2 3 4&1:Two fwd steps and a fwd lock under raised arms LRLRL End in Closed Position | 1 and 1/8 to R over 6 to 10
Lady may use Extended Forward Walk on 6. This is a 3-point turn.
count "2": Lady steps LF diagonally to Man's L side. 1/8 turn to R. When prepping leg to go diagonal, head looks along the diagonal line (not at Man), that's the spotting direction. Keep head, turn shoulder first, then head last and do half turn.
(Arms: start straight out to side, when turning under Man's arm, L arm will come in to turn, then immediately straight out at end of "2&".)
Count "3": RF brushes LF (for fast music, no time to brush, Forward Walk Turn go directly onto the foot, compact), 1/2 turn. L arm out. It's important to really get on to RF immediately. (Settle R hip right away before stepping LF on next step.)
On last half "&", continue to twist and turn upper body o R (but spine should continue to transfer to whole and front of foot) , then fast LF action to follow, do not place LF next to RF and pause, LF should directly place where it needs to go (chasse or lock depending on finishing position.)
count "4&1": Finishing to Man's R side: small step, but need to move hips, arms be quiet (arms is connected with Man).Do not put LH back on Man's shoulder too quick, wait till end of "1&" turning.
When following Closed Hip Twist, this lock step fwd passes Man.
continue small lock step forward (work hips), first build tone/pressure/energy with Man in body and arms, before 1/2 turn in Hip Twist.
After 1/2 turn, body weight still fwd, suck belly, create nice straight line with R leg.
How to tell if it's going to be a side chasse or forward lock for "4&1":
At the end of "3&": weight is on RF, do not anticipate what Man will do, also do not turn and hold LF next to RF and then step to next step. 
What Lady should do is: slight delay to read the lead, upper body start turning to R first, LF stays on floor as long as it can. Use Flexible Turns (where upper body goes first and delay lower half).
Note:
Finishing positions:
Man and Lady end to side
Man and Lady end diagonally fwd in Open CPP
Man 1/8 to R on step 8 arresting Lady's turn with L hand. Lady 1 1/8 R over 5-8
Lady end fwd toward Man's R side
Man turns body slightly R on step 8 to lead Lady towards his R side.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF into fwd Lock
Man LF slightly leftwards on 7.
Man and Lady fwd toward partner's R side
Man RF fwd in line with LF on 8, into fwd Lock towards Lady's R side. Lead, placement of LF on 7 and Lady's steps as position 3.
Man may make up to 1/4 R over 8-10, adjusting Lady's turn accordingly.
Gold: Alemana may be danced with R to R hand hold.
Precede with Hockey Stick or any figure ending in Open Position.
Man changing Lady's R hand into his R hand on last step and release hold with L hand.
Lady makes a complete turn R on Alemana.
Follow with the Advanced Hip Twist, Man changing Lady's R hand into his L hand, releasing hold with R hand on 7.

This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Many Cha Cha figures can be danced ith Man and lady using the same foot after Man having danced a Foot Change to get to Right Side, Right Shadow, or Tandem Position. There are different methods for doing foot changes.
Method 1 From Open Position to Right Side or Right Shadow Position (Normal opposite foot to same foot as partner)
From Open Position to Right Side Position:
Man:
2 3 4& 1: Dance Open Basic Movement for steps 1-5.
2: RF back
3: Transfer weight to LF
4: RF diag fwd
1: LF fwd and across, moving toward Lady's L side and turn 1/2 to R, to end LF back and slightly to side in RSP or R Shadow Position.
Now Man and lady dance RF back into Open or Closed Basic.
Lady: Lady does normal Open Basic Movement for steps 1 ~ 10.
From Open Position to Tandem Position, Lady behind.
Man:
Same as above, except instead of "RF diag fwd", step straight forward RF in line with LF, to end with LF back in front of Lady. No hold.

Method 2 From Right Side or Right Shadow Position to return to Open Position (Same foot to normal opposite foot)
Both start with weight on LF.
Man: dance the foot change steps described in Method 1 to get out.
Lady: dance normal Open Basic Movement.

Open Basic Movement (steps 6-10)
Method 3 From Open Position to Right Side or Right Shadow Position (Normal opposite foot to same foot as partner)
Man:
2 3 4& 1: Dance Open Basic Movement for steps 1-5.
2: RF back
3: Transfer weight to LF
4& 1: RF diag fwd Lock RLR, moving towards Lady's L side. (step straight fwd if intending to get into Tandem Position)
2: LF fwd and slightly across, towards Lady's L side and turning 1/2 to R, to end LF back and slightly to side in RSP or R shadow Position
3: Close RF to LF without weight (Ball of foot)
4&1: Rf to side to chasse RLR
Lady: does normal Open Basic Movement for steps 1 ~ 5, then Closed Basic with no turn for steps 6-20.

Lady: 1-5 Open Basic. 6-10 Closed Basic.

Dance Central - Advanced Hip Twists
Rumba Advanced Hip Twists
Rumba Advanced Hip Twists is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
2: LF fwd in RSP | Slight body turn to R
Previous step: lead lady towards R side.
Increase tone and lower L arm to R at chest level. Widen R arm in h old to R side.
3: Transfer weight to RF
4 1: LF ehind RF (Cuban Cross) | 1/8 to L (LF), body turns less
Retain tone and return arms to normal position, slightly to R
This is Pressed Backward Walk.
2: RF back | Slight body turn to L to normal position
3: Transfer weight to LF | Body completes turn
Increase tone in L arm. Release R hand hold.
4 1: RF to side in Fan Position | 1/8 to L over step 5 and 6
Slightly extend L arm, L hand still.
Follower
2: RF back in RSP | 1/2 to R
Turn starts end of previous beat. 
At previous beat: LF fwd in line with RF, R hip open up (which gives Man space), keep RF behind, keep pressure on RF more, press into it.
Do sharp 1/2 turn R, 2 options to finish (personal preference):
Option: after the turn, keep RF is in front, keep pressure on ball of RF, then quick release of RF, step back on count "2". Do not leave RF trailing during turn.
Option: during the quick turn, R toe/ball comes to the heel of LF, brushing/passing and quickly place back and then "melt" to R hip.
3: Transfer weight to LF
"3 &", on "&" start turning 1/2 to L to face Man. This is a bigger twist action. Weight onto LF, R hip lifted in the back, big preparation. 
Track RF when turning.
4 1: RF fwd in line with LF towards Man's R side | 5/8 to L
Turn starts end of previous beat. RF cross LF in front.
Pressed Forward Walk. Settle L hip more. make gap between legs bigger.
"4 1 &", do not turn to R early, Hold on 1, straighten R leg with weight, then turn on "&".
2: LF fwd passing in front of Man | 1/4 to R
Turn starts end of previous beat
Turn is initiated from hips. Hips turn more than body.
3: RF fwd and slightly across, then turn to end RF back. | 1/8 to L, then a further 1/2 to L. Body turns less.
Sharp turn
4 1: LF back in Fan Position | body completes turn
Do not let body weight take over. Release L leg and step back ahead of body. Backward walk action.
Fan position is a picture pose position. Give it some "significance" with more arm styling. Start from LH in middle of torso, then elbow-wrist-hand extend out. Body turns to left in the direction of the hand, do not tilt shoulders. Look at your audience, along the L arm.
Note:
Alternative finishing positions:
Open Position: Man turn 3/8 to L over steps 5 and 6.
Open CPP
Contact Position: use normal hold.
Man turn 1/2 to L over steps 5 and 6 to end RF to side and slightly fwd.
Lady: 3/4 to L over steps 5 and 6 to end LF behind RF as Cuban Cross.
Man can use "Press Line" on step 1 by making a slight swivel to R on RF and placing LF fwd on ball of foot with pressure but with partial weight.
This can be danced following Alemana with R to R hand hold, changing to L to R hand hold on step 5 of this figure.

Rumba Closed Basic Movements
Closed Position. 
Turn: No Turn, 1/8 to 1/4 turn are allowed. 1/8 Turn is described below.
Leader
2: LF fwd
Body lift up, Check/settle on "a". Check before switching direction, as late as possible.
3: Transfer weight on RF | 1/8 to L over 2-3
On "a", collect LF to closer to RF before stepping to side.
4 1: LF to side and slightly back
Hold on 4 1, rotating hips, on "a", collect RF, step back.
2: RF back
3: Transfer weight to LF | 1/8 to L over 5-6
4 1: RF to side 
Follower
2: RF back
See Latin Technique and Rumba Technique for more details.
"Out"- Separate with partner. settle more, but make sure to have forward tone to Man, not pulling Man toward you.
Squeeze/compress the side of the standing leg. (R leg at for this.)
Twist right side to cause LF to move forward, pendulum hip action diagonally.
3: Transfer weight on LF | 1/8 to L over 2-3
Continue pendulum action forward.
"In" - close up with Man, really show weight transfer to front of the foot, L hip high (really make sure to dance this fully), Squeeze L side, then twist, simultaneously RF to side.
4 1: RF to side 
Some coaches say this would be "side and slightly forward, not directly to the side".
Feel the energy in upper body.
Hip pendulum diagonally forward to R. Squeeze R side.
Transfer weight to RF. Twist right side to cause LF fwd.
2: LF fwd
Sharp, quick foot movement on previous "&". This is Forward Checked Walk.
Hip pendulum diagonally forward to left. Split weight with flat foot, then
On &, allow hip to "finish" (Finish all the way with spine over the foot), then twisting to neutral position. Twisting causes R leg knee checking action, with R knee goes to the spot. RF 90 degree arch.
3: Transfer weight to RF | 1/8 to L over 5-6
RF ball flat.
Hip is laterally to right. Then twist to move LF to side
4 1: LF to side and slightly back
R side twist to move L leg to side. L hip will be pendulum diagonally fwd (yes, fwd)
Compress into left side, at the same time, RF start moving back (continuing with closed basic.)
Note:
One should feel a continuous figure 8 action all the time.
Closed Basic Movement may end with taking step 6 in Open CPP, Man turning 1/8 R, Lady 1/8 L, Man release R hand hold.
Silver & Gold: Turn 3/8 to L over 4-6 to get to Contact Position.
step 3: RF side and slightly fwd. Lady LF behind RF as Cuban Cross.
Follow with 4-6 or 4-9 of Reverse Top.

Dance Central - Alemana
Rumba Alemana
This is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
2: LF fwd | No turn
3: Transfer weight to RF
4 1: Close LF to RF
Switch weight on '1'.
2: RF back
3: Transfer weight to LF
Man could do a side step; then step 6 would be a RF forward
4 1: Close RF to LF. End in Closed Position
Follower
2: Close RF to LF | No turn
To get into Fan position from previous 41: weight is more forward to Man (tension/pressure) after turning on 3&. before stepping LF back on 4. Hands hooked with Man's hand. More elastic feeling. Creates dynamic.
In Fan position, R arm/hand needs to be lower about belly button height, not too high.
When closing RF to LF, R side will squeeze, R arm will have pulling tension with Man.
L arm wrap in front of tummy, finish the arm action and don't cut it short. 
3: LF fwd
4 1: RF fwd | 1/8 to R
L arm out to side
Previous 2 can hold a bit longer and then make 341 faster toward Man, pressure into his hand, weight forward.
2 3 4 1: Three Fwd walks under raised arm LF RF LF. End in Closed Position | 1 1/8 to R over 4 to 6
On previous 1&: turn to R
Lady steps in a equilateral triangle.
"2&": LF step fwd to Man's L side, diagonally. Draw in L arm to front of body. Weight onto LF, On "&", RF brush (draw crescent moon) and point out (away from Man, no weight yet) to 2nd point in the triangle (middle in front of man)
"3&": step on RF, on "&" turn upper body to R, hold LF as long as possible, then sharp leg movement to place L leg.
"4": LF fwd step (could be to the side depending on next figure). Need to finish this step, settle weight with chest forward toward Man before next step. 
Note:
This is really a signature figure for Lady. Make it count!
Lady may use Extended Forward Walk on step 4.
Alemana may start in Fan Position or Open Position (Lady steps back on 1 instead of closing RF to LF, make a complete turn R over 4-6)
Alternative Finishing positions:
May and Lady end to side (could be in closed position)
Man steps in front of Lady, forcing her to step side instead of front.
Another option is to finish in Open Position: last step Man steps fwd, strongly hold hand and have Lady step back.
Man and Lady end diagonally fwd in Open CPP
Man 1/8 to R, on 6 stop Lady's turn with L hand, Lady 1 1/8 R over 3-6.
Lady end fwd towards Man's R Side (normal for continuing with Closed Hip Twist) - sort of true Alemana.
Man turns body slightly R on 6 to lead lady towards his R side. Lady LF fwd in line with RF.
Man LF slightly leftwards on the next step.
Man and Lady fwd towards partner's R side
Man RF fwd in line with LF towards Lady's R side
Lead next and last step as position 3.
Man may take up to 1/4 R over 5 & 6, adjusting Lady's turn accordingly.
R Shadow Position
Man leads Lady towards R side as above
Continue to turn her R.
Lady ends LF back having made 1/2 turn R on LF during beat 1. Gain R Shadow Hold.
Alemana can be danced with R to R hand hold. Precedes with Hockey Stick or any figure ending in Open Position.
Then Man changes Lady's R hand into his R hand on last step and release hold with L hand.
Lady makes a complete turn R on Alemana.
Man takes last step fwd i line with LF towards partner's R side.
Lead next and Lady's last step as # 3 position.
Follow with Advanced Hip Twist, man changing Lady's R hand into his L hand, release hold with R hand hold on 5.

Dance Central - Closed Hip Twist
Rumba Closed Hip Twist
This is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Start in Closed Position. Normally ends in Fan Position.
2: LF to side in RSP | Slight body turn to R 
Previous step: lead lady towards R side.
Turn frame to R, still a side step. Allow R hand/arm to slide down the lady's arm a little to give her more room for the turn and step. To the Lady, it appears Man in more "in front and left side, perpendicular" (vs. in Natural Opening Out or Opening Out to L and R, Man appears to be on the "side" of Lady.)
3: Transfer weight to RF
4 1: Close LF to RF | Very slight body turn to L
Switch weight on "1"
2: RF back | Slight body turn to L to normal position
3: Transfer weight to LF | No turn
4 1: RF to side in Fan Position 
Man could do slight turn to L to get into Fan position.
Follower
2: RF back in RSP | 1/2 to R
Turn starts end of previous beat. 
At previous beat: LF fwd in line with RF, keep RF behind, keep pressure on RF more, press into it. Body weight more fwd with Man, create push tension before the fast turn, RH pressure into Man's LH. 
Turn shoulder first, then turn twist hip sharp for fast 1/2 turn to R, now RF is in front. RF is turned out, lift the heel up. Do not leave RF trailing during turn. RF and R leg should have nice long leg line. then place RF back.
"Give" Man right arm, keep tone in arm, energy forward.
3: Transfer weight to LF 
"3 &", on "&" start turning 1/2 to L to face Man. Dance L hip fwd fully before turning.
Keep foot pressure on floor, track RF while turning. Turning shoulder/body first. 
4 1: RF fwd in line with LF towards Man's R side | 1/2 to L
Pressed Forward Walk. Turn starts end of previous beat. RF in strong demi-pointe position, RF more cross in front of LF, LF needs to swivel more, Settle L hip more, make gap between knees bigger.
Option 1: "4 1 &", do not turn to R early, Hold on 1, straighten R leg with weight, then turn to R on "&".
Option 2: straighten and turn at the same time on "1" for more energy.
2: LF fwd passing in front of Man | 1/4 to R
Turn starts end of previous beat. after turning, don't pause LF, place it and transfer weight
Turn is initiated from hips. Hips turn more than body.
3: RF fwd in line with LF, then turn to end RF back | 1/2 to L, body turn less
RF step fwd, really wrap R arm around belly tight, do not turn body too early. Then sharp 1/2 turn. Forward Walk Turn. Do not let body weight take over. 
After turning, there is still weight on LF (LF in front after turning), body weight fwd, keep tone/tension in RH with Man, before stepping LF back on "4".
4 1: LF back in Fan Position | body completes turn
Release L leg and step back ahead of body. Backward walk action.
Fan position is a picture pose position. Give it some "significance" with more arm styling. Start from LH in middle of torso, then elbow-wrist-hand extend out. Body turns to left in the direction of the hand, do not tilt shoulders. Look at your audience, along the L arm.
Note:
Alternative Finishing Positions:
Open Position: Man turn 1/4 L over 5 & 6
Open CPP:
Man turn 1/8 L over 5 & 6, leading lady to overturn on 5, both taking 6 diag. fwd.
Lady turns an additional 1/8 L on 5 using loosely crossed spiral action.
Contact Position:
Man turn 1/2 L over 5 & 6 to end RF side and slightly fwd.
Lady 3/4 L over 5 & 6 to end LF behind RF as Cuban Cross.
Normal hold retained throughout.

Cucaracha is 3 steps ended with feet together.
Start in Closed Position. See Rumba Technique for details on hip motion in Cucarachas.
Leader
2: LF to side with part weight. 
This is LF Cucaracha.
3: Transfer weight to RF | Flat Foot.
4 1: Close LF to RF
2: RF to side with part weight 
This is RF Cucaracha
3: Transfer weight to LF | Flat Foot.
4 1: Close RF to LF
Follower
2: RF to side with part weight. Flat foot.
This is LF Cucaracha (from Man's perspective).
Place foot faster, arms should be soft. Keep L heel down on the floor. 2 heels down.
3: Transfer weight to LF | Flat Foot.
R heel up, pushing and extend RF. RF turned out a bit more. At t his point, L hip is quite forward, R hip rotated back, RF turned out. 2 straight legs.
4 1: Close RF to LF
Switch hip fast, draws RF in.
2: LF to side with part weight
This is RF Cucaracha.
3: Transfer weight to RF | Flat Foot.
4 1: Close LF to RF.
Notes 
Feet relative direction to each other: side, side and slightly fwd or back, fwd or diagonally fwd, back or diagonally back.
Gold level: A Press Line may be used by Man when dancing a fwd Cucaracha by allowing RF to swivel slightly to R as he steps a fwd LF in line with RF, on ball of foot with heel raised.
L knee is flexed, R leg straight and RF flat.
There is no hip movement.
The Press Line may be used on 1 of Advanced, Continuous and Circular Hip Twists.

Rumba Cuban Rocks
This is a Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
See Rumba Technique for details on hip motion.
Cuban Rocks is 3 weight changes while keep feet in place and apart. Can be used when Man or Lady does Spot Turn or Switch Turn.
Normal hold, L to R hand hold, no hold. Try to keep both knees straight for all steps.
May use rotary or lateral hip action.
LF Cuban Rock may end in Open PP (slight turn L on 3, lady 4).
RF Cuban Rock may end in Open CPP (slight turn R on 3, Lady L)
Cuban Rock can be danced in LSP as an ending to Aida.
